methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.3B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.1B - $8.0B.

data.narrative:
Aalyria, a Series B space and aerospace company founded by Chris Taylor, is valued at $1.3B post-money following $130M+ in cumulative fundraising. The valuation assessment draws from a peer set of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage private companies and 5 public space and aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x to the $1.3B base, yielding $2.5B. Peer-median anchoring across four same-sector comparables—Sierra Space ($8.0B Series C), Cowboy Space Corporation ($2.0B Series B), Varda Space Industries ($1.5B Series C), and Skyryse ($1.1B Series C)—produces a $1.8B median valuation. The public-comp method cannot be applied due to absence of Aalyria's revenue figures, though the broader public sector trades at a 25.8x median P/S multiple across six tickers.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B to $2.8B, with a median of $2.1B. This implies 15–115% upside from the current $1.3B valuation. Material uncertainties include wide dispersion in the peer set ($1.1B to $8.0B) and the inability to anchor to public comparables without revenue disclosure. Stage-progression and peer positioning suggest the $1.8B–$2.5B band as most defensible.
